message:
⚡ Optimize AlertsContext: batch O(rules×alerts) evaluation into O(1) state update (#4788)

During evaluateConditions, each evaluate* function was calling setAlerts
individually inside loops — if 100 conditions matched, React processed
100 sequential state updates and 100 concurrent HTTP notification requests,
blocking the main thread for 200-500ms.

Refactor to collect all mutations (create/update/resolve) into a batched
accumulator during the evaluation cycle, then flush them in a single
setAlerts call via the new applyMutations() function. Notifications are
also batched with concurrency-limited HTTP requests via
settledWithConcurrency.

Key changes:
- Add MutationAccumulator type and applyMutations() pure function
- createAlert and queueAutoResolve push to accumulator during eval cycle
- evaluateConditions flushes all mutations in one setAlerts call
- sendBatchedNotifications replaces N individual HTTP requests
- All 236 existing tests pass unchanged

Fixes #4785

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
